exemplar.title: To what extent have Japan Tobacco International's risk reduction products improved its CSR and reduced stakeholder conflict?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent have Japan Tobacco International's risk reduction products improved its CSR and reduced stakeholder conflict?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

files.fileName: Final check Final Business IA Ruben.pdf

strengths: Consistent, clear use of the ethics concept to frame analyses (CSR, STEEPLE, stakeholder)
Strong organizational connectionâevery data point and example refers to JTIâs context
weaknesses: The ethical dimension is not woven into every analytical layer (e.g., ratio discussion)
Needs deeper linkage of financial outcomes back to specific ethical performance indicators
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand evidence places the work firmly in the 4-mark band: the key concept of ethics is applied throughout, showing strong understanding and clear analytical connection to JTI. It falls short of the full integration required for 5, as some sections (notably the ratio analysis) are only loosely tied back to ethics.

strengths: Four documents provided, meeting the quantitative requirement
weaknesses: Depth and academic rigor are limitedâmost analysis relies on only two sources
Inclusion of non-scholarly blog material dilutes credibility
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the correct number of documents is present, the evidence base lacks depth and scholarly balance. Documents are generally relevant but superficial, justifying a low band-2 descriptor rating (scored here as 1 to reflect very limited analytical use).

strengths: Selection of CSR matrix, STEEPLE, and ratio analysis shows an understanding of relevant tools
weaknesses: Applications are surface-levelâSTEEPLE and ratios lack benchmarking or deeper interpretation
Methodology description is incomplete, obscuring data-collection rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student employs appropriate frameworks but with only minimal depth or clarity of application. This performance aligns with the lower end of the 3-mark band, thus meriting a score of 1 to reflect very basic, uneven tool application.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Analysis connects financial trends to reputation and stakeholder trust
Use of concrete data (revenue figures, meeting counts) anchors the evaluation
weaknesses: Counterarguments and long-term implications are underdeveloped
Lacks triangulation and benchmarking for deeper insight
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation offers some effective analysis with correct data usage, matching the descriptor for âsome analysisâ in the 3-mark band. It is coherent but limited in breadth and depth, preventing higher marks.

strengths: Conclusion explicitly answers the research question
Summarizes key findings on CSR improvements and stakeholder relations
weaknesses: Fails to integrate counter-evidence or acknowledge limitations
Provides little evaluative nuance beyond restatement of results
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the conclusion addresses the research question, it is a minimalist summary lacking critical evaluation or engagement with conflicting evidence. This aligns with the lower band, warranting a score of 1.

strengths: Logical, coherent structure with clear headings and table of contents
Sections flow sequentially, mirroring the research outline
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates fully appropriate organization throughout, satisfying all descriptors for the top band in structure.

strengths: All required elements are present (title, word count, candidate code, headings, bibliography)
weaknesses: Numerous formatting inconsistencies (percentage notation, citation style)
Typographical and layout errors detract from professional polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the submission meets basic requirements, pervasive presentation errors inhibit clarity and professionalism, placing it in the lower band and meriting 1 mark.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure with a contents table, clear headings and sequential sections that mirror the outline, demonstrating appropriate organisation throughout and earning the top (2-mark) band for this strand.

The integrated report, earnings report and community-investment page provide solid, relevant data, while the external regulatory article broadens context.  Depth is adequate but not wide-ranging: most analysis draws on only two of these sources and quotations or detailed figures are limited.  This meets the descriptor for ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the richer range expected for the top band.

The submission includes the title, research question, word count, candidate code, section headings, supporting-document list and works-cited section, so all required elements are present, justifying the full 2 marks for presentation.
Four supporting documents are listed and clearly labelled, fitting the 3-5 requirement.  Hence the strand reaches the top band for quantity.

Each tool is applied to JTI and linked to the research question, but the application is occasionally surface-level (e.g., STEEPLE bullet points lack depth; ratios are stated without benchmarking).  Relevance is generally clear yet analysis is not consistently deep, fitting the âadequately appliedâ 3-mark band.

The student uses basic financial statements, stakeholder-meeting counts and CSR spending figures, constituting **adequate** data selection.  Data are inserted correctly in calculations and tables, but breadth remains limited and there is little triangulation, placing the work in the 3-mark band.

The analysis explains trends (profit margin rise, improved current ratio) and links them to reputation and stakeholder relations, but depth is uneven: counter-arguments and long-term implications are thin.  This amounts to âsome analysisâ of the research question, matching the 3-mark descriptor.

The student explicitly answers the research question in the concluding paragraph, directly stating the extent of improvement in CSR and stakeholder relations.  Despite limited depth, the explicit answer satisfies the 3-mark descriptor.

Three appropriate toolsâCSR matrix, STEEPLE and ratio analysisâare selected, covering ethical, external and financial perspectives.  This represents an **adequate** rather than extensive toolkit, satisfying the 3-mark descriptor.

Conclusionsâthat RRPs improve CSR and reduce conflictâare broadly supported by the evidence presented, yet they overlook conflicting data (e.g., critics citing continued health risks) and provide little evaluative nuance.  This aligns with âsome conclusions consistent with the evidence,â meriting 2 marks.

The discussion is firmly centred on Japan Tobacco International: all examples, data points and stakeholder references relate directly to JTI.  CSR initiatives, financial ratios and regulatory pressures are analysed in the specific organisational context, demonstrating **strong but not seamless integration**.  Occasional generalisations (e.g., âtobacco industryâ comments without specific JTI evidence) prevent it from achieving the âcomprehensive, fully-integratedâ standard, so a 4-mark band is most appropriate.

The student consistently references the key concept of ethics in the introduction, within each analytical tool (CSR, STEEPLE and stakeholder sections) and in the conclusion.  Ethics is linked to harm-reduction, public-health obligations and brand reputation, showing a strong conceptual grasp.  However, while the concept is present in most sections, it is not woven deeply into every layer of analysis (e.g., the ratio discussion is only loosely tied back to ethical considerations).  Thus the work demonstrates a **strong understanding with partial integration**, matching the 4-mark descriptor rather than the fully-integrated standard required for 5 marks.

annotations.comment: The research question is prominently stated, providing a clear focus for the investigation and guiding the subsequent analysis.
Including the key concept âEthicsâ near the beginning effectively anchors the analysis and reminds the reader of the central theoretical lens.
The presence of a personal candidate code and word count meets submission requirements and enhances clarity of presentation.
The table of contents clearly outlines sections and page numbers, demonstrating coherent organization and helping the reader navigate the report.
The table cells for page numbers are very narrow, which impairs readability. Consider aligning text and numbers in distinct, wider columns.
The introduction effectively uses JTIâs 2021â2022 revenue figures to frame the organizational context, anchoring later analysis.
The methodology description stops mid-sentence, leaving data sources and collection methods unclear. Expand this section to fully describe the research design.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: To deepen the conceptual link, explicitly connect the observed revenue increases from RRPs to specific ethical performance indicators such as reduced harm metrics.
The CSR section neatly separates environmental and social initiatives and relates them to the ethics concept, demonstrating good analytical structure.
Quantitative data on environmental improvements (e.g., percentage reduction in plastic use) are missing; including these metrics would deepen the CSR analysis.
The social responsibility paragraph ends abruptly. Complete the thought and incorporate stakeholder criticisms to present a balanced evaluation.
Selecting STEEPLE analysis demonstrates an appropriate application of external environment frameworks to examine RRP impacts.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The social factor discussion cites UN goals but lacks JTI-specific adoption data. Incorporate company or region-level statistics for stronger relevance.
Expand the economic section by providing cost-benefit figures for RRP development and marketing to quantify economic viability.
The political and legal factors are well-explained, showing how regulation and compliance support JTIâs CSR strategy.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The ethical dimension remains high-level; discuss specific ethical frameworks or principles that underpin JTIâs RRP strategy.
Synthesize the STEEPLE findings by summarizing how political, social, and ethical factors interconnect to enhance JTIâs CSR outcomes.
The ratio analysis section provides clear definitions and formulae, establishing a solid foundation for financial evaluation.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Formula formatting is inconsistent; use LaTeX such as $$\text{Operating Profit Margin}=\frac{\text{Operating Profit}}{\text{Revenue}}\times100\%$$ for precision.
Include industry benchmark ratios to contextualize JTIâs performance and show whether improvements are competitive.
The ratio table contains â24.59%%,â a typographical error. Correct to â24.59%â to maintain professional accuracy.
Simplify the table header by removing empty <th> cells and placing the title directly above the table for clearer presentation.
Explicitly tie the financial improvements back to ethical CSR objectives to show how increased margins reflect harm-reduction success.
The stakeholder engagement analysis provides concrete meeting counts and project examples, effectively illustrating trust-building efforts.
The phrase âstrains its relationsâ likely miscommunicates intent. Consider revising to âstrengthens its relationsâ for clarity.
The conclusion effectively summarizes findings and directly addresses the research question, reinforcing the reportâs coherence.
To demonstrate critical evaluation, introduce counter-evidence or limitations in the conclusion to provide balanced insight.
Listing four supporting documents meets the quantity criterion, demonstrating appropriate research breadth.
URL citations lack consistent formatting (italics, punctuation). Standardize references according to a recognized style guide for professionalism.
The âOther works Citedâ list includes non-peer-reviewed blog posts and lacks academic rigor. Prioritize scholarly articles to enhance credibility.
Reference entries vary in style and completeness; apply a consistent citation format (e.g., APA or MLA) throughout to improve presentation.
